goal: Improve heading accuracy, odometry quality, straight-line tracking, and host-link protocol readiness while continuous upstream telemetry remains active.
deliverable: A diagnosis-backed firmware refinement set with governed artifacts, code changes, and explicit verification notes.
constraints: Preserve the existing project architecture and avoid a full navigation stack rewrite.
Keep USART3 host telemetry active while ensuring control remains usable.
Prefer code paths that can be statically justified from the current firmware.
acceptance_criteria: Heading estimation and straight-line control use a more coherent signal path than the current default configuration.
Host-facing odometry/telemetry fields better match motion-relevant state and reduce right-angle distortion risk.
Communication behavior is explicit about how continuous telemetry coexists with host commands.
product_acceptance_criteria: The chassis should track straighter under the same PS2 command input after the change set.
The host should continue receiving streaming data without freezing the control loop.
The exported motion state should be more suitable for mapping/navigation than the current payload.
manual_spot_checks: Drive the chassis straight under PS2 and observe whether left/right drift decreases.
Run host-side mapping with telemetry enabled and compare corner shape and accumulated odometry error before and after.
Verify that host communication continues while motion commands and telemetry are both active.
completion_language_policy: Do not claim field-level navigation accuracy is solved without on-board validation. Only claim the implemented control, telemetry, and protocol refinements.
delivery_truth_contract: Only claim code paths that were actually changed and statically checked. Report build limitations explicitly.
non_goals: Full SLAM-side algorithm changes on the host computer.
Per-wheel factory calibration from live measurement data that is unavailable in the current environment.
DMA/RTOS redesign of the entire communication stack.
inferred_assumptions: Current heading quality is limited partly because IMU usage is disabled by default in runtime configuration.
Current host telemetry is not yet a clean odometry contract and likely under-specifies the motion quantities needed for mapping.
Straight-line drift can be reduced in firmware by improving heading hold participation and per-side balance handling before any hardware-only conclusion.
